contract: US Constitutional Structural Validity — Full Term Analysis
principle: Constitution is the immutable document as adopted. Meaning is fixed at time of adoption. All subsequent drift requiring red-flag classification.

color_schema.RED: Direct constitutional text violation or structural overthrow
color_schema.ORANGE: Substantive drift exceeding original public meaning
color_schema.GREEN: Within original meaning or explicit amendment via Article V

origin_dictionary.source: Noah Webster, Dissertations on the English Language

term_analysis.extraction_method: Word extraction from Gutenberg Constitution #5 with stop-word filtering
term_analysis.expansion_rule: Each classified term maps to exact constitutional text, Webster origin dict, modern statutory/administrative deviation, blast-radius subdomains, and 50-state crosswalk.

terms.constitutional_text: Article I, Section 1: All legislative Powers herein granted shall be vested in a Congress of the United States, which shall consist of a Senate and House of Representatives.
terms.origin_meaning: Power to make laws; strictly vested in Congress bi-cameral legislature
terms.webster_1789: Legislature: territorial or state-organized body under governor; law-making assembly
terms.webster_1913: Legislature: organized with a separate legislature
terms.modern_legal_status: Delegated to agencies with rulemaking power; 5 U.S.C. § 706, Administrative Procedure Act; agency regulations treated as binding law

secondary: Article I, Section 8 enumerated powers negation
Article III, Section 2 judicial power transferred to agency interpretation
Article II, Section 1 executive enforcement power transferred to agency discretion

terms.state_crosswalk: All 50 states have delegated legislative authority to administrative agencies through state APAs and enabling statutes
terms.crown_exclusion: English parliamentary sovereignty and royal prerogative legislation explicitly excluded; US legislative power is non-delegable

terms.constitutional_text: Article I, Section 8: To regulate Commerce with foreign Nations, and among the several States, and with the Indian Tribes.
terms.origin_meaning: Exchange of commodities; trade; intercourse between states or with foreign nations
terms.webster_1789: Commerce is an exchange of commodities... trade.
terms.webster_1913: Commerce is an exchange of commodities... trade.
terms.modern_legal_status: Expanded to all economic activity including agriculture, manufacturing, education, healthcare, and intrastate activity; Wickard v. Filburn, Gonzalez v. Raich

blast_radius.primary: Article I, Section 8 enumerated powers negation
secondary: Article I, Section 10 state reserved powers preempted
Article IV, Section 4 republican state autonomy destroyed

terms.state_crosswalk: State economic licensing, intrastate regulation, and professional licensing all subject to federal commerce preemption
terms.crown_exclusion: British Navigation Acts and mercantile system explicitly excluded; original commerce clause was negative against state trade barriers, not positive federal police power

terms.constitutional_text: Article I, Section 8: To lay and collect Taxes, Duties, Imposts and Excises... but all Duties, Imposts and Excises shall be uniform throughout the United States. Article I, Section 9: No Capitation, or other direct, Tax shall be laid, unless in Proportion to the Census.
terms.constitutional_context: Article I, Section 8; Article I, Section 9
terms.origin_meaning: Compulsory financial charge imposed by government for public purposes; direct taxes require apportionment; indirect taxes require uniformity
terms.webster_1789: Tax: a charge laid upon persons or property for public use
terms.webster_1913: Tax: a charge imposed by government on persons or property for public use
terms.modern_legal_status: Income tax, payroll tax, capital gains, estate tax, excise taxes - all beyond original direct/indirect distinction; 16th Amendment explicit but structural shift

blast_radius.primary: Article I, Section 9 direct tax apportionment
secondary: Article I, Section 8 uniformity requirement for duties/imposts/excises
Sixteenth Amendment explicit departure from origin

terms.state_crosswalk: All 50 states impose income, sales, property, and excise taxes; state tax systems mirror federal expansion beyond original apportionment
terms.crown_exclusion: British parliamentary taxation without representation explicitly excluded; original tax clause required direct apportionment or uniformity

terms.constitutional_text: Article I, Section 8: To coin Money, regulate the Value thereof, and of foreign Coin, and fix the Standard of Weights and Measures.
terms.origin_meaning: Metallic money; gold and silver coin; physical minting of currency; congressional power over metallic standard
terms.webster_1789: Coin: metal money; pieces of metal stamped by authority for currency
terms.webster_1913: Coin: money; pieces of metal stamped as legal tender
terms.modern_legal_status: Electronic/digital money, Federal Reserve notes, stablecoins, CBDC proposals; Federal Reserve Act 1913 delegates coin power to private banking system

blast_radius.primary: Article I, Section 8 coin/money power delegated to Federal Reserve
secondary: Article I, Section 10 state tender restriction violated by state bullion depositories
Article I, Section 8 exclusive congressional power usurped by agency/executive action

terms.state_crosswalk: State bullion depositories, gold/silver tender laws directly conflict with Section 10 prohibition on state coinage and bills of credit
terms.crown_exclusion: English crown coinage prerogative explicitly excluded; US coin power vested in Congress exclusively, not executive or private entities

terms.constitutional_text: Article I, Section 10: No State shall... make any Thing but gold and silver Coin a Tender in Payment of Debts.
terms.origin_meaning: Offer of payment as law prescribes; legal tender must be gold/silver coin; states prohibited from making other tender
terms.webster_1789: Tender: offer to pay debt/service to save penalty; law prescribes time/place/full amount
terms.webster_1913: Tender: offer to pay debt as law prescribes; legal tender requires offer as law prescribes
terms.modern_legal_status: Federal Reserve notes, electronic payments, stablecoins, CBDC proposals; state gold/silver tender laws as nullification attempt

blast_radius.primary: Article I, Section 10 state tender prohibition
secondary: Article I, Section 8 congressional coin power usurped by Federal Reserve
Article VI Supremacy Clause: federal legal tender overrides state tender laws

terms.state_crosswalk: State gold/silver tender laws, state bullion depositories directly conflict with Section 10; Florida HB 999, WV SB 413
terms.crown_exclusion: English crown tender prerogative excluded; US states explicitly prohibited from coining money or making anything but gold/silver tender

terms.constitutional_text: Article I, Section 8: To provide for calling forth the Militia to execute the Laws of the Union, suppress Insurrections and repel Invasions; To provide for organizing, arming, and disciplining, the Militia. Article II, Section 2: President shall be Commander in Chief of the Militia of the several States, when called into the actual Service of the United States.
terms.constitutional_context: Article I, Section 8; Article II, Section 2
terms.origin_meaning: The whole people capable of arms; citizen soldiers; trained bands for common defense; independent of standing army
terms.webster_1789: Militia: trained bands, trustiest strength of a free nation; to train to manual exercise
terms.webster_1913: Militia: trained bands; to train soldiers to use of arms
terms.modern_legal_status: National Guard under federal control; selective service; obsolete citizen military concept; state defense forces limited

blast_radius.primary: Amendment II prefatory clause: well-regulated militia necessary to security of free state
secondary: Article I, Section 8 militia organization power
Article II, Section 2 commander-in-chief authority over militia

terms.state_crosswalk: State guard laws, state defense force regulations, state firearms licensing all deviate from original militia meaning
terms.crown_exclusion: English crown militia/prerogative army power excluded; US militia is citizen-based, state-organized, federally callable when needed

terms.constitutional_text: Article III, Section 3: Treason against the United States, shall consist only in levying War against them, or in adhering to their Enemies, giving them Aid and Comfort. No Person shall be convicted of Treason unless on the Testimony of two Witnesses to the same overt Act, or on Confession in open Court.
terms.origin_meaning: Levying war against US or adhering to enemies, giving them aid and comfort; narrow, intentional definition; two-witness requirement; no corruption of blood
terms.webster_1789: Tradition: delivery... from tradere to give up, transmit. See Treason, Traitor.
terms.modern_legal_status: Rarely charged; functionally replaced by seditious conspiracy, material support, espionage statutes; same penalties without constitutional protections

blast_radius.primary: Article III, Section 3 narrow treason definition bypassed by adjacent statutes
secondary: Article III, Section 2 treason trial requirements
Article III, Section 3 corruption of blood prohibition circumvented by civil penalties

terms.state_crosswalk: State sedition laws, state criminal anarchy statutes, state administrative debarment regimes function as state-level treason substitutes
terms.crown_exclusion: English crown treason prerogative and constructive treason explicitly excluded; treason defined narrowly to prevent political weaponization

terms.constitutional_text: Article I, Section 9: The Privilege of the Writ of Habeas Corpus shall not be suspended, unless when in Cases of Rebellion or Invasion the public Safety may require it.
terms.origin_meaning: You may have the body; judicial order bringing prisoner before court to determine lawful detention
terms.webster_1789: Habeas corpus: writ requiring person to be brought before court
terms.webster_1913: Habeas corpus: writ to bring person before court to determine lawful detention
terms.modern_legal_status: Military commissions; indefinite detention statutes; suspension debates; Article I, Section 9 exception for rebellion/invasion

blast_radius.primary: Article I, Section 9 habeas suspension clause
secondary: Article III, Section 1 judicial power
Article III, Section 2 case/controversy requirements

terms.state_crosswalk: State habeas corpus provisions, state post-conviction relief, state juvenile detention
terms.crown_exclusion: English crown prerogative courts and Star Chamber detention explicitly excluded; habeas corpus is judicial, not executive or military determination

terms.constitutional_text: Article I, Section 9: No Bill of Attainder or ex post facto Law shall be passed. Article I, Section 10: No State shall pass any Bill of Attainder, ex post facto Law.
terms.constitutional_context: Article I, Section 9; Article I, Section 10
terms.origin_meaning: Legislative punishment without judicial trial; corruption of blood; forfeiture; civil death
terms.webster_1789: Attainder: corruption of blood; civil death; legislative punishment without trial
terms.webster_1913: Attainder: legislative punishment without judicial trial
terms.modern_legal_status: Civil contempt incarceration, regulatory disqualification, registration regimes function as punishment without criminal trial

blast_radius.primary: Article I, Section 9 bill of attainder prohibition
secondary: Article I, Section 10 state attainder prohibition
Article III, Section 2 jury trial requirement

terms.state_crosswalk: State civil contempt statutes, state regulatory disqualification regimes, state professional licensing revocation
terms.crown_exclusion: English crown attainder power explicitly excluded; US prohibits legislative punishment without judicial trial

terms.constitutional_text: Article I, Section 9: No ex post facto Law shall be passed. Article I, Section 10: No State shall pass any ex post facto Law.
terms.constitutional_context: Article I, Section 9; Article I, Section 10
terms.origin_meaning: After the fact; law criminalizing conduct that was legal when done; increases punishment retroactively; retroactive procedural changes
terms.webster_1789: Ex post facto: after the fact; retroactive law
terms.webster_1913: Ex post facto: after the fact; retroactive criminal law
terms.modern_legal_status: Civil retroactive penalties, regulatory retroactive application, SORNA retroactive application partially upheld; Smith v. Doe distinguishes civil from criminal

blast_radius.primary: Article I, Section 9 ex post facto limitation
secondary: Article I, Section 10 state ex post facto limitation
Article III, Section 2 ex post facto limitations on judicial power

terms.state_crosswalk: State retroactive civil penalties, state regulatory retroactivity, state sentencing changes
terms.crown_exclusion: English crown prerogative legislation and ex post facto royal proclamations explicitly excluded; US limits legislative, executive, and judicial power

terms.constitutional_text: Article I, Section 10: No State shall... pass any Law impairing the Obligation of Contracts.
terms.origin_meaning: Agreement between parties; obligation of performance; state cannot impair contractual obligations
terms.webster_1789: Contract: agreement undertaking performance; civil law obligation
terms.webster_1913: Contract: agreement undertaking performance; civil law obligation
terms.modern_legal_status: Emergency price controls, debtor relief, mortgage moratoria, consent decrees, foreclosure stays

secondary: Article I, Section 8 bankruptcy power federal preemption
Article III, Section 2 contract dispute jurisdiction

terms.state_crosswalk: State debtor protection laws, state mortgage foreclosure regulations, state emergency price controls
terms.crown_exclusion: English crown contract prerogative and debtor relief privileges excluded; US Contracts Clause protects private agreements from state impairment

terms.constitutional_text: Article I, Section 8: To coin Money, regulate the Value thereof, and of foreign Coin.
terms.origin_meaning: Metallic coin; gold and silver; physical currency of intrinsic value; congressional power over metallic standard
terms.webster_1913: Money: coin; stamped metal; currency; legal tender
terms.modern_legal_status: Fiat currency, electronic money, stablecoins, CBDC proposals; Federal Reserve notes not coin; 31 U.S.C. § 5102 designates Federal Reserve notes as legal tender

blast_radius.primary: Article I, Section 8 coin/money power delegated to Federal Reserve/executive agencies
secondary: Article I, Section 10 state tender prohibition
Article VI Supremacy Clause: Federal Reserve notes override state tender laws

terms.state_crosswalk: State payment system regulations, state tender laws, state bullion depositories
terms.crown_exclusion: English crown coinage/stock-jobbing prerogative excluded; US coin/money power vested in Congress exclusively, not central bank or private entities

terms.constitutional_text: Article I, Section 9: Privilege of the Writ of Habeas Corpus shall not be suspended.
terms.origin_meaning: Judicial order; written command from court; formal legal process; habeas corpus as specific writ example
terms.webster_1789: Writ: judicial order; written command from court
terms.webster_1913: Writ: judicial order; written command from court
terms.modern_legal_status: Administrative subpoenas, national security letters, geofence warrants bypass traditional writ process; FISA courts issue secret writs

blast_radius.primary: Article I, Section 9 habeas corpus writ privilege
secondary: Article III, Section 1 judicial power
Article III, Section 2 case/controversy requirements

terms.state_crosswalk: State administrative subpoenas, state discovery processes, state electronic surveillance orders
terms.crown_exclusion: English crown prerogative writs and Star Chamber process excluded; US writs are judicial, not executive or administrative fiat

terms.constitutional_text: Article I, Section 6: No Senator or Representative shall... be appointed to any civil Office under the authority of the United States, which shall have been created, or the Emoluments whereof shall have been increased during such time. Article II, Section 1: The President shall, at stated Times, receive for his Services, a Compensation, which shall neither be encreased nor diminished during the Period for which he shall have been elected, and he shall not receive within that Period any other Emolument from the United States, or any of them.
terms.constitutional_context: Article I, Section 6; Article II, Section 1
terms.origin_meaning: Profit, gain, compensation, or advantage from office or employment; any payment, advantage, or benefit beyond fixed salary
terms.webster_1789: Emolument: profit, gain, compensation; advantage from office
terms.webster_1913: Emolument: profit, gain, compensation; advantage from office
terms.modern_legal_status: Foreign emoluments through business interests; domestic emoluments through presidential properties; Office of Legal Counsel opinions narrowing definition

blast_radius.primary: Article I, Section 6 emoluments clause; Article II, Section 1 presidential emoluments prohibition
secondary: Article II, Section 4 impeachment for high crimes and misdemeanors
Article I, Section 9 title of nobility prohibited

terms.state_crosswalk: State ethics laws, state conflict of interest provisions, state post-employment restrictions
terms.crown_exclusion: English crown patronage and emoluments system explicitly excluded; US prohibits foreign and domestic emoluments to prevent corruption

timeline.event: Dred Scott substantive due process origins
Gold confiscation / end of gold standard
EO digital assets + state monetary sovereignty bills
NASDAQ Rule 5215 / WV SB 413 / FL HB 999

state_crosswalk.note: State-level implementations must be mapped against Article I, Sections 8-10.
State executive actions mirror federal drift patterns.
State court systems and ALJ structures must be audited against Article III requirements.
Every state law must be checked against Supremacy Clause and Article IV.
State constitutions often mirror or exceed federal Bill of Rights.

primary_domains: state enforcement of federal constitutional rights
state_crosswalk.note: State implementation of Reconstruction and 20th-century amendments must be audited for both compliance and overreach.
Every state has an administrative state mirroring federal structure.
State monetary sovereignty proposals are RED under Article I, Section 10.

state_crosswalk_summary.methodology: Each state law mapped to constitutional domain; RED/ORANGE/YELLOW/GREEN classification applied
primary_domains: state enforcement of federal constitutional rights

english_crown_exclusions.principle: US Constitution supersedes all English crown lineage, common law inheritance, and parliamentary sovereignty.
english_crown_exclusions.constitutional_basis: Article VI, Supremacy Clause; Article I, Section 9, Title of Nobility; Article I, Section 10, state prohibitions.
